Now, implementing this in C++.

The failure function can be computed as follows:

vector<int> computeFailure(const string &t) {
    int n = t.size();
    vector<int> fail(n, 0);
    for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
        int j = fail[i-1];
        while (j > 0 && t[i] != t[j]) {
            j = fail[j-1];
        }
        if (t[i] == t[j]) {
            j++;
        }
        fail[i] = j;
    }
    return fail;
}

Then, in the solution:

string shortestPalindrome(string s) {
    if (s.empty()) return s;
    string rs = s;
    reverse(rs.begin(), rs.end());
    string t = s + '#' + rs;
    vector<int> fail = computeFailure(t);
    int l = fail.back();
    string add = s.substr(l);
    reverse(add.begin(), add.end());
    return add + s;
}

solution:
```class Solution {
public:
    string shortestPalindrome(string s) {
        if (s.empty()) return s;
        string rs = s;
        reverse(rs.begin(), rs.end());
        string t = s + '#' + rs;
        vector<int> fail = computeFailure(t);
        int l = fail.back();
        string add = s.substr(l);
        reverse(add.begin(), add.end());
        return add + s;
    }
    
private:
    vector<int> computeFailure(const string &t) {
        int n = t.size();
        vector<int> fail(n, 0);
        for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
            int j = fail[i-1];
            while (j > 0 && t[i] != t[j]) {
                j = fail[j-1];
            }
            if (t[i] == t[j]) {
                j++;
            }
            fail[i] = j;
        }
        return fail;
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n)

space complexity: O(n)

energy implications: LOW

explanation: The solution efficiently finds the longest prefix palindrome using the KMP failure function approach, ensuring minimal character additions to form the shortest palindrome.
